data.title: Monport Laser launches Mega S CO2 Laser Engraver in response to small businesses' increasing demand for precision, automation, and ease of use. Winter months spur need for streamlining operations.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, in New York, there is a rising need for laser engraving solutions among small businesses as they optimize operations for the upcoming year during the winter season. This demand is driven by the desire for precision, automation, and user-friendliness in these solutions. This trend is specifically observed in the Mega and Mega Lite CO2 models, reflecting a shift towards efficiency and advanced technology among businesses.

data.title: Zylox-Tonbridge to acquire equity interest in a European medical device company, accelerating global expansion and adoption of innovative solutions.
data.description: Zylox-Tonbridge has reached a significant achievement in their dedication to global growth by establishing a comprehensive international commercialization platform. This move aims to expedite the adoption of Zylox-Tonbridge's pioneering vascular solutions on a global scale. The company has successfully integrated manufacturing and operational capabilities to support this expansion effort, indicating a strong step towards enhancing their presence in the global market.

data.title: Discovery Silver Corp. produced 66,718 ounces of gold in Q4 2025, totaling 180,424 ounces since acquiring Porcupine on April 15, 2025, and 234,702 ounces for full-year 2025.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, Discovery Silver Corp. revealed that its Porcupine operations yielded 66,718 ounces of gold in Q4 2025. Since acquiring Porcupine on April 15, 2025, the company has produced a total of 180,424 ounces by the end of the year. Adding in the gold production prior to the Acquisition, Porcupine operations generated a sum of 234,702 ounces for the entirety of 2025. All figures mentioned are in US dollars unless specified otherwise. Discovery Silver Corp. is listed on the TSX under "DSV" and on the OTCQX under "DSVSF".

data.title: Rubicon Organics grants 1,002,773 RSUs and 1,002,773 PSUs, marking Canada’s top organic cannabis producer.
data.description: Vancouver, BC, Jan. 15, 2026 - Rubicon Organics Inc. disclosed the issuance of 1,002,773 restricted share units (RSUs) and 1,002,773 performance share units (PSUs). Operating as Canada’s top premium licensed producer specializing in organic-certified, premium, and super-premium cannabis items, the Company intends to enhance its operations with these allocations. Rubicon Organics Inc. trades publicly on the TSXV under the ticker symbol ROMJ and on the OTCQX as ROMJF, solidifying its position in the market.

data.title: Nexo fined $500K by CA regulator for issuing 5,500 loans without license, failing to assess repayment ability.
data.description: The financial regulator in California has alleged that Nexo provided around 5,500 loans to residents of California without a valid license. Furthermore, the company did not adequately evaluate the borrowers’ repayment capacities.

data.title: Crypto prices dip after bill stalls; BTC at $95,485.03, ETH $3,306.90, XRP $2.07, DOGE $0.1397; market selloff due to Senate opposition.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies experienced a dip following the stall of a key cryptocurrency bill in the Senate due to opposition from industry giants. Bitcoin was down 1.29% at $95,485.03, Ethereum dropped 0.82% to $3,306.90, XRP fell 2.66% to $2.07, Solana decreased by 2.75% to $142.06, and Dogecoin saw a decline of 4.23% to $0.1397. Bitcoin had hit a 2-month high of $97,000 before dropping, with its trading volume falling by 13% in 24 hours. The Senate Banking Committee postponed markup on the bill, causing sell-offs in the market. Cryptocurrency-related companies like Strategy Inc. and Coinbase Global Inc. closed down by 4.70% and 6.48%, respectively. Over $320 million was liquidated from the cryptocurrency market.
